content:
Same  issue  rork RORK — APK AND EXPO GO STARTUP FAILURE: FIX THE REAL ROOT CAUSE

I tested the new APK on a real Android device.

Observed result:

1. IVX splash screen appears.
2. After approximately 5 seconds, the app displays:
   “IVX is taking longer than expected.”
3. After that, the app becomes a permanent black screen.
4. Owner Login never renders.
5. The APK and Expo Go are therefore NOT working.

The 5-second timeout screen is not a fix. It only exposes that startup initialization is still hanging.

STOP all other development.

Do not modify owner login again until the application can render the first route reliably.

PHASE 1 — CAPTURE THE REAL FAILURE

Reproduce the exact failure using the same production Android APK.

Capture:

- adb logcat from 10 seconds before launch until 30 seconds after the black screen
- JavaScript console logs
- native Android exception logs
- unhandled promise rejections
- Expo Router initialization logs
- React render errors
- startup state transitions

Return the first real exception or unresolved startup operation.

Do not return “slow network” unless logs prove it.

PHASE 2 — AUDIT THE EXISTING STARTUP CHAIN

Audit the actual existing files and architecture, including:

- app/_layout.tsx
- lib/auth-context.tsx
- lib/supabase.ts
- all providers mounted by the root layout
- splash-screen initialization
- font loading
- asset loading
- AsyncStorage initialization
- Supabase getSession()
- Supabase onAuthStateChange()
- INITIAL_SESSION processing
- profile or owner-role lookup
- API health checks
- remote configuration
- navigation redirects
- startup timeout implementation
- error boundary
- retry button implementation

For every startup operation, document:

- file
- line number
- function
- whether it blocks rendering
- timeout behavior
- failure behavior
- resolved, rejected or permanently pending status

PHASE 3 — REMOVE BLOCKING STARTUP DESIGN

The root navigator must render without waiting for network-dependent work.

Required startup order:

1. Native application mounts.
2. Root error boundary mounts.
3. Local loading screen renders.
4. Local auth state initializes with a strict timeout.
5. Login or public route renders.
6. Network-dependent data loads afterward.

Supabase, Render, remote APIs, profiles, members, analytics, realtime subscriptions and health checks must not block the initial route.

If auth initialization fails or times out:

- stop the spinner
- set session to null
- show Owner Login
- display a small diagnostic message
- do not show a black screen
- do not retry forever

PHASE 4 — FIX THE TIMEOUT AND RETRY LOGIC

The current “IVX is taking longer than expected” screen is broken because the application becomes black afterward.

Fix it so:

- Retry executes a real bounded initialization attempt.
- Retry cannot create duplicate listeners.
- Retry cannot clear the React navigation tree.
- Retry cannot leave loading=true forever.
- After the final failed attempt, Owner Login still renders.
- A diagnostic code is visible.
- The exact sanitized startup error is logged.

Never return null from the root layout after the splash screen is hidden.

Never leave the root layout without a rendered screen.

PHASE 5 — ADD STARTUP TRACE LOGGING

Add a unique startup trace ID and log these checkpoints:

- APP_MOUNTED
- ROOT_LAYOUT_RENDERED
- PROVIDERS_MOUNTED
- SPLASH_HIDE_STARTED
- SPLASH_HIDE_COMPLETED
- AUTH_INIT_STARTED
- AUTH_INIT_COMPLETED
- AUTH_INIT_FAILED
- ROUTER_READY
- INITIAL_ROUTE_RENDERED

Every checkpoint must include elapsed milliseconds.

The missing checkpoint will identify where startup stops.

PHASE 6 — VERIFY CONFIGURATION

Verify the APK contains valid production values for:

- EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
- EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- API base URL
- Expo Router entry configuration

Do not expose secret values.

Return only:

- variable name
- PRESENT or MISSING
- source used during build
- runtime validation result

Check for development-only URLs such as:

- localhost
- 127.0.0.1
- 10.0.2.2
- local IP addresses
- expired tunnel URLs
- loca.lt
- ngrok development URLs

A production APK must not depend on Metro or a local computer.

PHASE 7 — REQUIRED REAL-DEVICE QA

Test a clean Android installation:

1. Uninstall the old IVX application.
2. Clear application storage.
3. Install the new signed APK.
4. Disable Wi-Fi and launch.
5. Confirm a usable login or offline screen renders.
6. Enable Wi-Fi and launch.
7. Confirm Owner Login renders.
8. Force-stop and reopen.
9. Background and foreground the app.
10. Press Retry after a simulated startup error.
11. Repeat launch 10 times.
12. Confirm zero black screens.
13. Confirm zero infinite loaders.
14. Confirm zero startup crashes.

Also verify Expo Go separately.

Do not claim APK success based only on Expo Go.
Do not claim Expo Go success based only on a web preview.

REQUIRED EVIDENCE

Return:

- exact root cause
- first failing log entry
- file and line number
- code change
- files changed
- TypeScript result
- Expo diagnostics result
- Android logcat excerpt
- startup trace showing INITIAL_ROUTE_RENDERED
- clean-install video
- screenshot of Owner Login
- GitHub commit SHA
- Android build ID
- new APK link
- APK checksum
- confirmation the APK was tested on a real Android device

DEFINITION OF DONE

The task is complete only when a clean production APK reliably opens to Owner Login or a usable offline screen.

The following are automatic failures:

- black screen
- permanent splash screen
- “taking longer” followed by black screen
- infinite spinner
- app depending on Metro
- app depending on a temporary tunnel
- retry button that does not recover
- claims without logcat and real-device evidence
